title: Daemon integration, role YAML, and heartbeat migration
description:
Wire up automation engines in the daemon, load from role YAML, and remove the old heartbeat system. From docs/design-triggers-schedules.md.

Sections: Daemon Integration, Role YAML, Heartbeat Migration, Persistence and Lifecycle

Key deliverables:
- Role struct additions: Triggers []TriggerSpec, Schedules []ScheduleSpec
- Role YAML parsing for triggers/schedules sections with validation
- Template rendering of trigger/schedule action strings via existing role template engine
- Daemon startup: create ActionRunner, TriggerEngine, ScheduleEngine, load from role, start goroutines
- Event fan-out from monitor to TriggerEngine (separate channel, non-blocking)
- Resume behavior: re-load role-defined automations on --resume
- DELETE internal/session/heartbeat.go and heartbeat_test.go
- Remove HeartbeatConfig from Role, remove RunHeartbeat goroutine from daemon
- Make old heartbeat: YAML key a validation error
- Integration tests: role YAML trigger fires on event, schedule fires on time,
  heartbeat-as-schedule works at idle priority

References:
- Plan: docs/design-triggers-schedules.md §Daemon Integration, §Role YAML, §Heartbeat Migration, §Persistence and Lifecycle
- Depends on: h2-ifg.4 (CLI/socket protocol — listener handlers needed)
